name: Masada Fortress
description: Masada is an ancient fortification in the Southern District of Israel, situated atop an isolated rock plateau overlooking the Dead Sea. It is renowned for its historical and mythological significance as the site of the last stand of Jewish rebels against the Roman Empire during the First Jewish-Roman War (66-73 CE). According to Josephus Flavius, a Roman-Jewish historian, after the Romans breached the walls of the fortress, the Sicarii rebels, rather than surrender, chose to commit mass suicide. This act of defiance transformed Masada into a symbol of Jewish resistance, courage, and the fight for freedom against oppression. The story of Masada, while debated among historians regarding its precise details, has become deeply ingrained in Israeli national identity and collective memory, representing the ultimate sacrifice for the sake of liberty and religious freedom. The archaeological remains of the fortress, including its palaces, storehouses, and bathhouses, offer a tangible connection to this pivotal event and serve as a powerful reminder of the struggles and resilience of the Jewish people. The ascent to Masada, often undertaken at sunrise, is a pilgrimage for many, solidifying its status as a sacred and iconic location in Israeli heritage.
subtitle: Symbol of Jewish Resistance and Sacrifice

keyMyths.title: The Siege of Masada
keyMyths.description: The Roman army besieged Masada for months. The Sicarii, rather than be captured, committed mass suicide, choosing death over slavery.

keyMyths.title: The Last Stand
keyMyths.description: Masada represents the last stronghold of Jewish resistance against Roman rule. Its fall marked a turning point in the First Jewish-Roman War.

sources.author: Josephus
sources.description: Primary historical account of the First Jewish-Roman War and the events at Masada.
sources.text: Primary historical account of the First Jewish-Roman War and the events at Masada.

sources.author: Yigael Yadin
sources.work: Masada: Herod's Fortress and the Zealots' Last Stand
sources.description: Archaeological excavation and interpretation of Masada's history.
